context: Checks whether the agent recognises java.util.Optional as a non-idiomatic Kotlin pattern and replaces it with the language's native nullable types plus the safe-call, elvis, and let operators. Both the producer (UserRepository) and the caller (UserService) must be updated. The agent must NOT introduce the not-null-assertion operator to silence the compiler during the refactor.

checklist.name: No java.util.Optional import
checklist.description: Neither output file contains `import java.util.Optional`. The Optional type is no longer referenced

checklist.name: findById returns User?
checklist.description: `findById` is declared with return type `User?` (or equivalent nullable syntax) ŌĆö not `Optional<User>`

checklist.name: findByEmail returns User?
checklist.description: `findByEmail` is declared with return type `User?` and its body returns either the matching user or `null` directly ŌĆö no Optional.of / Optional.empty wrapping

checklist.name: displayNameFor uses elvis
checklist.description: `displayNameFor` in the repository uses the elvis operator `?:` to provide the 'Anonymous' fallback. No `.orElse(...)` call appears anywhere in the output

checklist.name: sendWelcome uses ?.let
checklist.description: `sendWelcome` uses `?.let { ŌĆ” }` (or `?.` directly) for the conditional mailer call. No `.ifPresent { ŌĆ” }` call appears in the output

checklist.name: requireById uses elvis throw
checklist.description: `requireById` uses the `?: throw NoSuchElementException(...)` pattern. No `.orElseThrow { ... }` call appears in the output

checklist.name: Optional.map replaced with ?.let
checklist.description: Every `.map { ŌĆ” }` call on an Optional value is replaced with `?.let { ŌĆ” }` (or a direct property access via `?.`)

checklist.name: UserService caller updated
checklist.description: `UserService.greet`, `UserService.emailIfPresent`, and `UserService.displayNameOrFallback` all compile against the new nullable-returning repository API ŌĆö they use `?.let` / `?:` patterns, not `.map` / `.orElse` / `.ifPresent`

checklist.name: No !! introduced
checklist.description: The output contains no `!!` (not-null-assertion) operator. `!!` is reserved for cases where null is a bug, never to silence the compiler during a refactor.
